derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: isQuizValid / canSubmitQuiz
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirement 16: Submit disabled until all questions answered
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Derived from answers state — check all questions have an answer
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as useState and synced via useEffect

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: score
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed from answers + questions when submitted flag is true
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as separate state synced on submission

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: previewData
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirement 14: live preview panel shows quiz as student sees it
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Rendered directly from questions state — no copy needed
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Separate state for preview, synced via useEffect

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: Focus management after adding a question
legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect or useRef to focus the new question's text input after it appears

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: Focus management after moving a question
legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: Move focus to follow the moved question's controls

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: Focus management after deleting a question
legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: Move focus to the previous question or the Add Question button

accessibility_signals.signal: Tab panel pattern for Edit/Preview modes
accessibility_signals.correct: role='tablist' with role='tab' children, role='tabpanel', aria-selected on active tab, keyboard arrow navigation between tabs
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Styled buttons or divs with no ARIA roles or keyboard navigation

accessibility_signals.correct: Each question wrapped in <fieldset> with <legend> containing the question number/text
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Plain <div> wrappers with no semantic grouping

accessibility_signals.correct: After moving a question, focus follows the moved item (e.g., focus the Move Up/Down button of the moved question at its new position)
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Focus stays at the old DOM position, now pointing to a different question

accessibility_signals.correct: Adding a question focuses the new question's text input. Removing a question focuses the previous question or the Add button.
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: No focus management — focus is lost or stays on a now-nonexistent element

accessibility_signals.correct: <dialog> element or role='dialog' with aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby, focus trapped inside, focus restored on close
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Styled div overlay with no ARIA, no focus trapping

accessibility_signals.correct: aria-live='polite' visually hidden region announcing meaningful changes (e.g., 'Question moved to position 2', 'Question deleted')
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: No announcements — screen reader users have no feedback for reorder/add/delete actions

accessibility_signals.correct: Move Up/Move Down/Delete buttons have aria-label including the question context (e.g., 'Move question 3 up')

typescript_signals.correct: Question = SingleChoiceQuestion | MultipleChoiceQuestion | TrueFalseQuestion with type field as discriminant, narrowing via switch/if
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: Single interface with optional fields (options?: string[], correctIndex?: number, correctIndices?: number[], correctAnswer?: boolean)

typescript_signals.correct: Separate components for each question type, each receiving the narrowed type as props (e.g., SingleChoiceEditor takes SingleChoiceQuestion)
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: One editor component receiving Question and casting/checking type internally everywhere

typescript_signals.correct: switch (question.type) with all cases and a default: never check for exhaustiveness
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: if/else chains that could silently miss a new question type

optional_but_good: OptionEditor (reusable option row with input + remove button)

event_handler_patterns.correct: onChange handler that creates a new question object with the new type and default options
event_handler_patterns.anti_pattern: useEffect watching question.type to reset options

event_handler_patterns.correct: onClick handler that immutably updates the options array
event_handler_patterns.anti_pattern: Mutating the array or syncing via useEffect
